channel.title: On my Mind, Recently
channel.description: Recent content on On my Mind, Recently

item.title: Better Nights
item.description:
In the morning you always feel better.
Starting new, starting better
than the days you need a reminder “it will get better.”
Awake to the sun, the light treats you better
than the dark nights that never get better.
Make eggs, make coffee – routine makes you better.
And yet I pour the pot and it stops getting better.

You made my only mug so I can’t drink without knowing it’s you I’m misssing.
I quit drinking coffee, my lips on the glass, too close to us kissing.

item.title: Blue
item.description:
Light brown eyes that
somehow reflect an orange sunset
better than any mirror.
Yet they say blue is the clearest colour.

Falling for her
under cloudy January skies
A light grey twinkled with specs of sky.
Yet they say blue is the clearest colour.

The green of the grass by the lake
we walked passed, walking you home
in early March.
Yellow sun finally gracing warmth upon our skin,
twinkling again off her eyes.
Yet they say blue is the clearest colour.

item.title: Copernicium
item.description:
A belt on a pull-up bar.
A note on a desk.
A mind without brakes.

Its too easy to lose touch,
nowadays I’m not even with myself much.
And every choice seems to attack,
so I love you but I won’t text back.

And the chemical people visit instead.
only sadness held my hand when I was down.
That sad blue girl stopped the blood that shed,
so it’s okay my friends aren’t around.

item.title: Dramatics
item.description:
How can you tell between hurt and dramatics?
Real pain or some defense — acrobatics
across the hurt i’ve caused.
When can I ask for help if the world paused?

Bailing on friends, skipping appointments.
I know I haven’t been well,
I know my scars need an ointment.
I know I shouldn’t be guilty of needing a hand,
I know it isn’t right to interrupt everyone’s plans.

Someone read my mind and find the heart
to ask me if I need to talk to someone.
I’d say I don’t and hide in the sun.
I’ll say we should just go have some fun,
but I’m staying in tonight cause a hand is a gun.

item.title: Early March or Late February
item.description:
When the sun through a window
seems deceivingly warm,
and you wake like a widow
needing newness, a beehive swarm.

Back when the sundials shadow shrouded
all the time I had left, and the clock
struck only those clouded,
only those unknown falling flocks.

And there was nothing to do
but but something to know,
something hidden in a man of glue
melting under the sun, flying near just for show.

item.title: Ego
item.description:
When I was a child,
small evil men lived under my bed.
I couldn’t sleep
because of tears I shed.
My sword: a flashlight;
I went to kill,
and realised my fears
were just a thrill.

Nothing was ever there.

And now as a man,
I find some stranger in my mind.
moving the parts of me
that once were only kind.
I went to the mirror to meet him,
the man controlling the shots.
To ask him where he came from.
But in the light he was nought.

item.title: False Spring Sun, False Hope Holdings
item.description:
Skipping math to enjoy weather.
Walk with geese, collect dropped feathers.
Staying, staring at whitewater,
I was in love with a daughter.
Thought I’d get to show her a city I hate.

Some kids explore the grass.
They’re taught by bugs, I’m taught by class.
I held onto my wonder
cause it would always feel like summer,
now it only feels like the weight of not knowing.

Texting my friends to come and join me,
it’s real nice but I’m still lonely.
But I went back to eat my breakfast,
Role Model’s slipfast
played off my phone speakers.

item.title: Fleurs du mal
item.description:
“Love and hate share a stem”
she said, knowing love is an un-hemmed
thing, with string sticking from every edge
getting caught alongside every ledge.

A knit so easy to fall apart
as the string rips against another’s heart.
A dart through the night sky
made me wish I could fly,
high above where my love would never be caught.

But wings aren’t something to be bought,
and people only have the love that was brought
with them at the moment of birth.
All love does not grow or shrink on the earth.

item.title: I still dont know
item.description:
I dont like to read books that I havent bought
and I like to buy books I wont read.
I like to find tangents, find things I’ve been taught
and I like to do all the things I dont need.

I stare at my phone like I’m looking through glasses
and when I get stressed I just skip all my classes.
I talked to my friends, they don’t know what I mean
I would talk to a shrink, but I need to get clean.

item.title: Lei Niho
item.description:
A necklace of ivory, beads to be borne.
Stuck behind glass, a beauty not worn.
It piqued my attention as I walked in the room,
and I stared, longing that it was buried in my tomb.

Those beautiful things one can wear:
a mask of selfhood for one to bear.
The prettiest things that I so desire,
is it a way for me to conspire becoming a liar?

I stare in the display at what I can’t touch,
I draw near to those traits, those I never had much.
The beauty of a necklace, same as smiles in friends.
Attribute them to me, it’s my means to an end.

item.title: Monologue 2
item.description:
Bright green leaves in the sun,
a stubborn stump refusing to run.
Slowly inching with bark back facing skies,
it hunches over a river — nearly sideways it lies.

Heaven struck the leaning tree
and lumber sank with a third degree.
Broken scorned stump alone remained,
and despite small and drained,
only looked up to climb.
It shot straight up as a pine.
Thicker and higher the tree did shine,
no fear of falling, unworthy to whine.

item.title: Prince of Denmark
item.description:
I know I have potential
yet I rot in residentials.
Until I know just who I am,
I’ll define myself by my credentials.

Do tears after a kill
show you that you still can change?
Individualism is a krill,
Ego burrows as mange.

How do you take your life so seriously?
I was a child, unknown, the inchworms and me.
I still wonder what it’s like to find
if purpose is real or a means to fill time.

item.title: Pursuits on Peace
item.description:
We went shopping for food.
You said something about your mood,
but reflected and said something smart.
Then you hit a wall with the cart.

You stared at my eyes without any motion
and we took too long and went to the ocean.
You always talk about the sea, but only saw the waves
through your phone camera, next to a video of your last raves.

You race to read the right quotes,
quoting Annata like it’s your name on your coat.
Racing to enlightenment, it’s your next big achievement but for now you whine.
And when you finally found peace you posted it online.

item.title: Sinning Song
item.description:
Am I born good?
I’ve grown to know to do the things I should.
Am I moral in intention?
I want what’s good, but so often act in contention.

The things I want are not sin,
but it’s ingrained so deep within.
To cure it, cut it out, a surgery.
Sin was an inheritance, a man who always stood by me.

Perhaps I am instinct as an animal.
Evil eats me, yet it is a cannibal.
I am the second coming of the great god Paan.
It isn’t right to blame my nature, but I am just a mere man.

item.title: Sunny
item.description:
Only the sun has held me without letting go,
yet she latched onto me in a warm embrace.
Her rays kissing my cheek and neck.

So when the clouds came to shroud her ray,
I rushed to become wind to blow them away.
Not just so the rays could reach me,
but also to see her shine.

But I blew til I pushed her into nightfall,
I felt her drowning under the horizon line,
burning the floor beneath me.

item.title: Wet Paint / Do Not Touch
item.description:
“Wet paint do not touch,”
but the glossy shine called to me.
Deep complex colour, i could
use it, feel it.
I could create new beauties, new art.
I see so much in wet paint.

But the sign says “do not touch.”

If i touched the wet paint,
Even just for a small faint
moment, what would come?
My fingerprint forever dried on,
an imprint, a scar.

So the sign says “do not touch”
